jokes.text: Oh, Casablanca always makes me cry. / Oh, good. I thought it was me.
jokes.setup: Lisa says the movie makes her cry
jokes.punchline: Will assumes he might be the reason she's crying
jokes.explanation: Simple self-deprecating humor that works for the character but isn't particularly original

jokes.text: Can I...? / Can I see you...? / Can I see you... again?
jokes.setup: Will hesitates asking something intimate-sounding
jokes.punchline: He's just asking for another date
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection structure - makes audience think he's asking something sexual

jokes.text: You know, I ain't talking about marriage or nothing, though.
jokes.setup: Just asked for a second date
jokes.punchline: Immediately clarifies he's not thinking marriage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's youth and fear of commitment

jokes.text: Oh, do me, do me. / Hilary, you're August 18th. / Wow, you are good.
jokes.setup: Carlton offers to guess birthdays at carnival
jokes.punchline: Hilary is impressed he knows her own birthday
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Hilary's ditzy nature makes this land well

jokes.text: Come see the amazing Rock Dwarfsky... the world's shortest square-headed Negro.
jokes.setup: Carlton doing carnival barker routine
jokes.punchline: Describing himself as a carnival attraction
jokes.explanation: Self-aware physical comedy that plays to Carlton's earnest nature

jokes.text: What was he doing there?
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions Will left his book at the library
jokes.punchline: Family member shocked Will was at a library
jokes.explanation: Quick character-based joke about Will's academic habits

jokes.text: What? / I've seen this look before. The facial flush, the flare in his nostrils. Look at his eyes.
jokes.setup: Will acts casual about Lisa
jokes.punchline: Carlton analyzes Will's obvious attraction like a scientist
jokes.explanation: Carlton's clinical observation style applied to romance

jokes.text: We choose not to define our relationship.
jokes.setup: Family teasing about girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Will uses pretentious language to avoid admitting feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will trying to sound sophisticated

jokes.text: God, I'm lonely.
jokes.setup: Praising Lisa's qualities
jokes.punchline: Sudden honest admission of loneliness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - unexpected vulnerability delivered deadpan

jokes.text: I wonder why you don't see a lot of black professional bowlers. / Ain't no money in it. Look... how much dough could a brother make endorsing a pair of these?
jokes.setup: Lisa wonders about black bowlers
jokes.punchline: Will points to ugly bowling shoes as the reason
jokes.explanation: Solid observational setup leading to endorsement joke

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Darnell Washington, pro bowler. Whenever I'm about to roll me a 300 game, I like to do it in these. Air Ghettos.
jokes.setup: Mock endorsement speech
jokes.punchline: Air Ghettos instead of Air Jordans
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of athletic endorsements with perfect product name twist

jokes.text: Girl, you just won yourself a date to a carnival with an ebony god. / Really? / I'd rather go with you.
jokes.setup: Will calls himself an ebony god
jokes.punchline: Lisa says she'd rather go with him (implying he's not the god)
jokes.explanation: Perfect comeback structure that deflates Will's ego smoothly

jokes.text: Excuse me, Dark Gable.
jokes.setup: Confronting Will
jokes.punchline: Dark Gable instead of Clark Gable
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but feels like a setup for the better comeback

jokes.text: Excuse me, Messy Snipes?
jokes.setup: Called Dark Gable
jokes.punchline: Messy Snipes instead of Wesley Snipes
jokes.explanation: Strong comeback with clever name corruption that fits the character

jokes.text: A what? A Power Ranger?
jokes.setup: Lisa about to reveal martial arts training
jokes.punchline: Will's pop culture reference to explain her fighting ability
jokes.explanation: Timely pop culture reference that lands but feels somewhat cheap

jokes.text: Baby, you were on the floor. / That's how I fight.
jokes.setup: Lisa points out Will was on the ground
jokes.punchline: Will claims that's his fighting strategy
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-based humor showing Will's pride and refusal to admit weakness

jokes.text: You, sir, with the big ears. How about testing your skill?
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to attract carnival customers
jokes.punchline: Insulting potential customer's appearance
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Carlton's lack of social awareness

jokes.text: You, sir, with the big ears. How about testing your skill?
jokes.setup: Carlton doing carnival barker routine
jokes.punchline: Insults potential customer's ears
jokes.explanation: Carlton's oblivious rudeness while trying to drum up business is typical of his character.

jokes.text: Got a noodle for an arm? / Carlton, that's Ken Griffey Jr., man.
jokes.setup: Carlton taunts customer about weak arm
jokes.punchline: It's a famous baseball player
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic setup with Carlton's obliviousness to celebrity status

jokes.text: Hi, quarterback. / How you doing? / I'm kind of chilly. Wanna take me to Hawaii? / Sorry, I'm married. / Then stop flirting with me.
jokes.setup: Hilary flirts with baseball player
jokes.punchline: When rejected, blames him for flirting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - self-absorbed logic and turning rejection into his fault

jokes.text: Will, you shouldn't be threatened by my knowledge of shotokan. / Whoa, whoa, whoa. First, I don't care if you know Chaka Khan. I ain't threatened by nobody.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions shotokan martial art
jokes.punchline: Will thinks she said Chaka Khan (singer)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mishearing joke that perfectly fits Will's character and creates quotable moment

jokes.text: First, I don't care if you know Chaka Khan. I ain't threatened by nobody.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions her knowledge of shotokan martial arts
jokes.punchline: Will mishears it as 'Chaka Khan' the singer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant malapropism that perfectly captures Will's street knowledge vs formal education gap

jokes.text: Carlton, can I confide in you? / I feel it too, Lisa. / Feel what? / Never mind.
jokes.setup: Lisa wants to confide in Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks she's expressing romantic interest, then backs down
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his romantic delusions and quick retreat when reality hits.

jokes.text: You'd rather a guy bash your face in... than to have a woman who likes this face... step in and save it? / Finally you understand.
jokes.setup: Lisa points out the illogic of Will's position
jokes.punchline: Will agrees with the absurd logic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing masculine pride overriding logic

jokes.text: He says he's at the store.
jokes.setup: Will tells Geoffrey to say he's at the store
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey deadpan repeats it to Lisa on phone
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry delivery makes this simple avoidance funny

jokes.text: If I was with a wimp who couldn't defend himself, I'd just leave.
jokes.setup: Will complaining about Lisa defending him
jokes.punchline: Hilary calls him a wimp and says she'd leave
jokes.explanation: Hilary's blunt assessment that makes Will's situation worse

jokes.text: Hello? Mr. Yoshi? Yiki-Yo, Yoshi?
jokes.setup: Will tries to call a martial arts instructor
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces and makes up Japanese-sounding words
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's cultural ignorance and desperation, though the humor is somewhat dated.

jokes.text: [IN CHINESE ACCENT] Chan, you have offended my family. Now I must avenge my brother's death.
jokes.setup: Will practicing martial arts alone
jokes.punchline: Doing bad martial arts movie impression with coffee table as opponent
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment showing Will's movie-influenced martial arts fantasies

jokes.text: Hey, listen, man, I wanna be just like Steven Seagal. / Oh, you wanna be a bad actor. Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Will wants to be like action star Steven Seagal
jokes.punchline: Martial arts teacher insults Seagal's acting
jokes.explanation: Good burn on Steven Seagal that subverts expectations of wise martial arts master

jokes.text: Yeah, that's cool, but can we hurry? I gotta whup somebody's butt quick.
jokes.setup: Master talking about patience and discipline
jokes.punchline: Will immediately shows no patience
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment contrasting Eastern philosophy with Will's impatience

jokes.text: Listen, can you teach me that thing... where you ram somebody's nose up into they brain?
jokes.setup: Will interrupts philosophical story
jokes.punchline: Asks about extremely violent martial arts move
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's impatience with philosophy and focus on violence. Dark but fits his desperation.

jokes.text: Without what? / I see symbolism is not one of your strong points.
jokes.setup: Master's philosophical dragon metaphor
jokes.punchline: Will completely misses the point, master gives up
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between philosophical wisdom and Will's literal thinking

jokes.text: Well, what did he do? / Last I heard, he was playing Diana Ross in La Cage aux Folles.
jokes.setup: Will asks what happened to guy in similar situation
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and absurd outcome
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and unexpected outcome that's completely absurd

jokes.text: Counterfeiters, extortionists, car salesmen.
jokes.setup: Jazz reading criminal categories
jokes.punchline: Car salesmen grouped with serious criminals
jokes.explanation: Classic escalation joke putting car salesmen with criminals

jokes.text: Wait a minute, this guy will let me punch him 10 times for 40 dollars? / Mention this ad, and he'll throw in a head butt.
jokes.setup: Will reading about fake fight service
jokes.punchline: Head butt as bonus offer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd criminal service with infomercial logic

jokes.text: Hey, man, where'd you get this from anyway? / I stole it.
jokes.setup: Will asks about the criminal directory
jokes.punchline: Jazz matter-of-factly admits theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz moment - casual admission of crime delivered deadpan

jokes.text: Fine, take the money. But then what? Holed up in some fleabag motel waiting for the man to come and take you away. And all that's left is to walk that last long, lonely mile with the Lord's Prayer... / Will you give the man the damn money?
jokes.setup: Phil philosophizing during robbery
jokes.punchline: Vivian's exasperated interruption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Phil's dramatic tendencies at worst possible time

jokes.text: Hey, look, point it right at me. Make it look real. / Huh?
jokes.setup: Will thinks this is his hired actor
jokes.punchline: Real robber confused by direction
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony - audience knows what Will doesn't

jokes.text: Jazz, you can take your fake cop. Lisa left. / Will, he's real. / And so is he.
jokes.setup: Will dismissing everything as fake
jokes.punchline: Everything was actually real
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the fake setup premise with escalating reality

jokes.text: Jazz, you can take your fake cop. Lisa left. / Will, he's real. / And so is he.
jokes.setup: Will complains about the fake setup after Lisa leaves
jokes.punchline: Jazz reveals both the cop and robber were real
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the fake robbery setup. Will's plan backfires spectacularly.

jokes.text: And all that for only 40 bucks?
jokes.setup: Arrested for interfering with real robbery
jokes.punchline: Will focuses on the money aspect
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate reaction focusing on the deal aspect

jokes.text: And all that for only 40 bucks?
jokes.setup: Will realizes he nearly got arrested for his scheme
jokes.punchline: Still focuses on the money aspect
jokes.explanation: Will's misplaced priorities even after disaster is typical of his character.

jokes.text: But usually I know why I be tripping, man, but this time I don't have no clue.
jokes.setup: Will admits he's been acting dumb
jokes.punchline: Says he usually knows why he's being stupid
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness humor. Will acknowledging his pattern of bad decisions is character growth disguised as comedy.

jokes.text: Well, no, but I seen The Flintstones, like, five times.
jokes.setup: Phil asks if Will took anthropology
jokes.punchline: Will cites cartoon as education
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's unconventional education

jokes.text: Yeah. I got plenty of dudes out there still looking for places.
jokes.setup: Phil's example about men giving wrong directions
jokes.punchline: Will admits he's done this multiple times
jokes.explanation: Self-aware callback that makes the abstract example concrete and personal

jokes.text: No, I guess I don't. / Then why didn't you just say so? / Because I'm a man. Hmph.
jokes.setup: Will catches Phil in his own example about men not admitting ignorance
jokes.punchline: Phil admits he's doing exactly what he described
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where the character proves his own point about male behavior

jokes.text: Shouldn't you be out fighting crime?
jokes.setup: Will approaches after the heroics incident
jokes.punchline: Lisa sarcastically references his failed heroics
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to earlier events with appropriate sarcasm

jokes.text: No, I'm crazy about you. There's a difference.
jokes.setup: Lisa calls Will crazy
jokes.punchline: Will turns 'crazy' into romantic declaration
jokes.explanation: Sweet wordplay that serves the romantic subplot well

jokes.text: I was trying to fight Akira's dragon. Only the dragon was from without. / Without what? / That's the same place the whole thing fell apart for me too.
jokes.setup: Will references the martial arts philosophy
jokes.punchline: Still doesn't understand it, admits confusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Will learned nothing from the philosophy lesson

jokes.text: Cool. But you know if we had the same job, I'm gonna still make more money.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggests they're equals
jokes.punchline: Will points out wage gap reality
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary that punctures the romantic moment with reality

jokes.text: Get a room.
jokes.setup: Will and Lisa kissing
jokes.punchline: Carlton's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard episode tag line that's predictable but serves its purpose
